code: fn leak() -> Entry<'?> { let t = table::new(); let e = t.entry(k); return e; }
why: R13: e's region is t's local block region, not a region parameter of leak; a token can never outlive its source's frame.

code: struct Holder { e: Entry<'h> }  // and: let bx = box_new(own e)
why: R1 rejects the non-confined declaration with a confined field (generic wrappers caught post-monomorphization); R2 rejects the heap store statement.

code: let e1 = t.entry(k1); let e2 = t.entry(k2);
why: R5: a uniq issue requires no entry overlapping t; (t,uniq,e1) is live. Same rule kills get-two-and-swap; the blessed route is a sealed two-key op.

code: let c1 = t.range(lo,mid); let c2 = t.range(mid,hi); let v = t.get(k); let n1 = c1.next(); let n2 = c2.next();
why: R5/R6 shr regime: shr issues and &-receiver ops coexist; both cursors auto-consume at scope end (R7).

code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); let e = t.entry(k);
why: R5: uniq issue while a shr entry lives on t; no upgrade path exists — consume the cursor first.

code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); let t2 = own t;
why: R6: a place overlapped by a live entry cannot be moved, own-passed, or dropped.

code: let g = m.lock(); match flag { T => { unlock_via(&m, own g); } F => { noop(); } }
why: R12: fall-through arms disagree on loans(m) and g's liveness; blessed shape consumes in both arms or in neither.

code:
fn reserve<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, k: K) -> Entry<'t> { let e = t.entry(k); return e; }
let e = reserve(&uniq tt, k); e.fill(v); tt.insert(k2, v2);
why: R9 tie ('t -> t) is well-formed; R10(b) derives the uniq issue at the call from the signature alone; the return-effect check (R10c) matches; fill consumes, insert then legal — the wrapped-writer bless

code: let e = t.entry(k); loop { e.fill(v); match done() { T => { break; } F => { noop(); } } }
why: R12: the reachable back edge's projected state (e dead, loans empty) differs from loop entry (e live, uniq entry on t).

code: loop { let k = next_k(); let e = t.entry(k); e.fill(v); match done() { T => { break; } F => { noop(); } } } t.insert(k9, v9);
why: R12's projection excludes body-locals after body scope-end processing, so back edge equals entry; issue/consume balance per iteration.

code:
fn transfer<'a,'b>(x: &'a ring<T>, y: &'b ring<T>, p: own Prod<'a>) -> Prod<'b> { retire(&x, own p); let q = y.producer(); return q; }
let pa = a.producer(); let pb = transfer(&a, &b, own pa); let r = a.endpoint_reset();
why: Honest composition, not laundering: R10(c) seeds (x,shr,p) in the body; retire consumes it; y.producer() freshly issues; return at caller-supplied 'b passes R13 and the exit-effect check; caller books

code: fn pick<'t>(x: &'t table<K,V>, y: &'t table<K,V>, sel: B, ...) -> Cursor<'t> { ... }
why: R9: result region 't ties to two borrow-mode parameters; rejected at declaration so source places stay singleton-exact (reject-when-unsure).

code: let c = t.range(lo,hi); loop { let n = c.next(); match n { Some(x) => { let v = t.get(x); } None => { break } } } let w = t.get(k2);
why: R6 shr regime permits &-receiver get under the live shr entry; back edge equals entry (R12); read-under-cursor typechecks.

code: let g = m.lock(); loop { match predicate(&g) { Done => { break } Wait => { let g2 = cv.wait(&m, own g); } } }
why: R12: Wait arm ends with g dead and holder g2 (auto-consumed at arm end, unlocking); projected back-edge/arm states disagree with entry (g live); iteration 2 would run unlocked on dead g.

code: let e = t.entry(k); e.fill(v); let e2 = t.entry(k2); e2.fill(v2); { let e3 = t.entry(k3); } let c = t.range(lo,hi); let x = t.get(k4);
why: R7 removes entries at explicit consume and at inner-scope auto-consume (e3's drop releases); sequential uniq sessions then a shr session compose.

R8 renames the entry's holder e->w at construction; R9 totality forces threading t (the original finish(w)-only signature rejects at declaration, zero ties); the call consumes under R6's sole-loan exc

code:
fn relock<'r>(m: &'r mutex<T>, g: own Guard<'r>) -> Guard<'r> { ... }
let g1 = m1.lock(); let gx = relock(&m2, own g1);
why: R10(a): g1's recorded source place m1 differs from the tied argument place m2; place identity is the brand and cannot be laundered.

code: let g = m.lock(); par::for_chunks(&uniq data, &g, body);
why: R14: a holder holding any uniq entry cannot pass a replicate-shared slot; duplicating unique interior access across bodies would be a race by construction.

code: fn gut<'t,'u>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'u>) { t.insert(k2,v2); e.fill(v); }
why: R9: 'u ties to zero borrow-mode parameters — undeclarable. The single-region rewrite gut<'t>(t, e: own Entry<'t>) declares, but R10(c) seeds (t,uniq,e) so the insert-before-fill body order rejects at

code:
fn launder<'t>(t: &'t table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { return e; }
let e2 = launder(&t, own e); let c = t.range(lo,hi); let n1 = c.next(); e2.fill(v1);
why: Kind travels with the confined type (R1/R10b), never with the tie's mode: e2 is reissued as a uniq entry, so t.range's shr issue rejects at R5; no &-tied slot can downgrade a uniq token to shr.

code: fn probe<'t>(t: &'t table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) { let c = t.range(lo,hi); let n = c.next(); e.fill(v); let n2 = c.next(); }
why: R10(c) seeds (t,uniq,e) at body entry; the shr issue t.range rejects at R5 — the callee frame can no longer forget the caller's loan.

code: fn refresh<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { t.insert(k9,v9); return e; }
why: Under the R10(c) seed, R6 rejects the insert (uniq entry live, statement does not consume e); a body of just { return e } is legal but harmless — an identity reissue over an unmutated source.

code: fn relocate<'t>(t: &uniq 't table<K,V>, e: own Entry<'t>) -> Entry<'t> { let e2 = t.entry(k2); return e2; }
why: R5 under the seed: a uniq issue while (t,uniq,e) is live; the front-door route to two live uniq tokens is closed.

code: let c1 = tt.range(a,b); let c2 = tt.range(c,d); close_one(&uniq tt, own c1); let n = c2.next();
why: R6's consume-exception requires the consumed holder's entry to be the SOLE entry on tt; (tt,shr,c2) is live, so the &uniq mint rejects and c2 cannot be dangled.

code:
let c = t.range(lo,hi);  // only token
t.delete_at(own c);      // delete_at(&uniq 't btree, own Cursor<'t>): consumes
why: R6 sole-loan exception: the statement surrenders the only token while mutating, atomic at statement granularity — no sibling exists to dangle; this recovers the SQLite delete-at-cursor shape.

code:
confined(uniq) enum MaybeTok<'r> { Some(Entry<'r>), None }
let none = MaybeTok::None<'r_tt>; let e2 = open(&uniq tt, own none);
why: R5: a user construction leaving any region parameter unbound by a captured field is rejected, so no loanless forged-region token exists; R10(a)'s must-hold-the-loan check would independently reject th

code: confined(shr) struct WView<'r> { p: &uniq 'r slot }
why: R1 coherence: a confined(shr) type transitively containing a &uniq field is ill-formed, so shared tokens can never carry unique interior write paths (protects OWN-5 and the optimizer's shr-window fact

code: confined(shr) struct Cloak<'r> { inner: Entry<'r> }  // Entry is uniq-kind
why: R1 coherence: shr wrapper containing a confined(uniq) field is ill-formed; kind can never be re-keyed by wrapping (R8 transfers never change kind).

code:
struct Db { t: table<K,V>, u: table<K,V> }
let e = db.t.entry(k); db.t.insert(k2,v2); e.fill(v);
why: Loans are place-keyed (R4): entry (db.t,uniq,e) freezes db.t and db (overlap) but not db.u; the insert rejects at R6 exactly like P2 — struct-held forms are fully supported, with fill-then-insert acce

code:
struct Two { a: ring<T>, b: ring<T> }
let pt = two.a.producer(); feed(&two.b, &uniq pt, v);
why: R10(a): pt's source place two.a differs from the tied argument place two.b; brands are resolved places, so one level of struct wrapping cannot bypass the P5 verdict.

code: let t1 = table::new(); let e1 = t1.entry(k1); e1.fill(v1); let t2 = table::new(); let e2 = t2.entry(k2); rebind e1 = e2;  // scope end follows
why: R7 checks the derived drop itself: at scope end t2 is reached while (t2,uniq,e1) is live — a checked error; safety no longer rests on binding-order position, which the rebind falsified.

code: match env { Env { t: tb } => { let tbl2 = own tbl; let v = tb.get(k); } }
why: R11: struct patterns never destructure confined values (only whole-token enum destructure exists), and R3 keeps field access statement-scoped, so no bare borrow binding can outlive the entry that guar

code:
confined(uniq) enum EntryResult<'t> { Occ(OccTok<'t>), Vac(VacTok<'t>) }
let r = t.entry(k); match r { Occ(o) => { o.replace(v); } Vac(v0) => { v0.fill(v); } }
why: R11: every variant packs its payload into exactly one same-kind, same-region token; the scrutinee's entry renames per arm and both arms consume, so R12 joins agree — the flagship hashbrown occupied/va

code:
confined(uniq) enum LockResult<'m> { Got(Guard<'m>), Busy }
let r = m.try_lock(); match r { Got(g) => {...} Busy => {} }
why: R11: the Busy variant carries zero token fields while the scrutinee holds an entry (issue is unconditional at the call); the blessed shape is the state-queryable token (next entry).

Retry-acquire crosses the loop by RETURN, not break: F arm consumes so the back edge equals entry (R12); the T-arm return passes R13 and R10(c)'s exit-effect check (entries on m = exactly the signatur

code: fn body(chunk: &uniq 'c chunkview<i64>, env: &'e Env<'e>) reads('e) writes('c) { ... }
why: R9 self-tie: no distinct borrow parameter carries 'e and env is by-borrow, so the signature is well-formed; self-ties seed nothing and skip the call-site source comparison (and cannot be re-passed int

code:
let c = t.range(lo,hi); loop { let v = c.next_view();  // form clause: issues View on source(receiver), shr
  ...view dies at body end each iteration... }
why: R5 holder-receiver issue: the loan lands on c's recorded source place as a sibling shr entry (t,shr,v); zero-copy scan views work without per-element re-lookup, preserving the B-tree/SQLite performanc

code: fn mk_env<'r1,'r2>(t1: &'r1 table<K,V>, t2: &'r2 table<K,V>) -> Env2<'r1,'r2> { let env = Env2 { lut: &t1, dict: &t2 }; return env; }
why: R9: each region ties to exactly one parameter; R10(b) derives one shr issue per tied argument place — multi-borrow env helpers compose, fixing the exactly-one-issuer wrong-rejection.

code: let g = m.lock(); let g2 = move g;
why: R8: a plain let-move renames the entry's holder to g2 (never drops or duplicates it); the unlock runs at g2's scope end, so the lost-release completion cannot be implemented.

code: let e = t.entry(k); loop { e.fill(v); break; }
why: R12: every body path ends in break/return, so the back edge is syntactically unreachable and only the (single) break state is checked — equivalent to the straight-line program.

code:
// form table: table.get declared & receiver, internals memoize the last lookup
par::for_chunks(&uniq data, &env, body);  // bodies call env.lut.get(k) concurrently
why: R15: an & receiver op on a loanable form must preserve interior addresses AND be safe under concurrent invocation; the memoizing declaration fails hostile fact-channel review, so no program lawfully l

code:
confined(uniq) struct Tok { }  // no region parameters
fn escape() -> Tok { let q = conc_queue::new(); let p = q.producer(); return p; }
why: R1: every loanable confined type must carry at least one region parameter; with it, R13 rejects the return (local region), so the queue can never die under a live endpoint.
